plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name mandate.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability, aligning with capital preservation.
Sell QQQ, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to exit flagged positions and eliminate all leveraged ETFs.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall equity exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a potential target of ~1.5% per month. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility and drawdowns, but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to align with the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, SMH, and SPAL to exit all flagged positions and reduce overall risk.
Trim SOXL to 5% to maintain minimal leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into a performing tech name.
Buy MSFU at 10% to add a strong, high-scoring scanner setup.
Maintain a cash buffer of approximately 10% by keeping total adds less than trims.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5% growth per month by blending broad index with quality tech and limited leverage. It reduces overall risk by exiting flagged positions and trimming concentration. Expect moderate volatility and growth, with a focus on risk-first management.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell QQQI, QQQ, SMH, and SPAL to exit all flagged positions and cut losses.
Increase CRWD to 25% to concentrate on a performing tech name, pushing the limit.
Add SOXL to 10% to leverage semiconductor upside, within the mandate.
Buy MSFU at 15% and TZA at 5% to add high-scoring scanner setups and some inverse exposure.
Maintain a smaller cash buffer of approximately 5% to maximize market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4% growth per month by leaning into leverage and concentration in high-scoring setups. It involves significantly higher risk, with potential for larger drawdowns due to leveraged ETFs and market volatility. Strict stop discipline is crucial, as leveraged ETFs decay daily and can lead to rapid losses.

moves.note: Reduce broad index for income focus

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to shift focus towards income-generating assets.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize monthly distributions.
Sell QQQ, CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and SPAL to eliminate high-beta names and all leveraged ETFs.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
Maintain a cash buffer to manage potential NAV fluctuations.
income.read: This income plan aims for approximately 1% per month in distributions by rotating into income-focused ETFs. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, cutting leverage and high-beta single names. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is not a savings account.
